dark:hover:text-sky-400"}]]}],["$","h2",null,{"id":"new-project-generator-behavior-project-creation-paths-and-names-in-nx-generators","className":"group scroll-mt-20","children":["NEW PROJECT GENERATOR BEHAVIOR: Project Creation Paths and Names in Nx Generators",["$","$Lb",null,{"aria-hidden":"true","href":"#new-project-generator-behavior-project-creation-paths-and-names-in-nx-generators","children":["$","svg",null,{"xmlns":"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg","fill":"none","viewBox":"0 0 24 24","strokeWidth":1.5,"stroke":"currentColor","aria-hidden":"true","data-slot":"icon","aria-labelledby":"$undefined","className":"mb-1 ml-2 inline h-5 w-5 opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100","children":[null,["$","path",null,{"strokeLinecap":"round","strokeLinejoin":"round","d":"M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"}]]}]}]]}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Alright, getting into the newest updates from Nx, the biggest change is coming in the form of Nx project generators."}],["$","p",null,{"children":["Originally, all Nx workspaces had a directory structure where applications were always held in an ",["$","code",null,{"children":"apps/"}]," directory, while libraries were held in the ",["$","code",null,{"children":"libs/"}]," directory."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["Over the years, we found this to be much too rigid, so we made these locations more configurable. We added a ",["$","code",null,{"children":"workspaceLayout"}]," category to the ",["$","code",null,{"children":"nx.json"}]," file, where you could set ",["$","code",null,{"children":"appDir"}]," and ",["$","code",null,{"children":"libDir"}]," options to customize the names of these directories."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["We also simplified the definition of Nx projects to be any directory that had a ",["$","code",null,{"children":"project.json"}]," file in it, allowing for the ability to nest projects in your filesytem and also forego app or lib directories altogether!"]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["With Nx 16.8, we're introducing an all-new option in this ",["$","code",null,{"children":"workspaceLayout"}]," category: ",["$","code",null,{"children":"projectNameAndRootFormat"}]," where you can chose either the option to apply project names ",["$","code",null,{"children":"as-provided"}]," or ",["$","code",null,{"children":"derived"}],"."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["$","img",null,{"src":"/documentation/blog/images/2023-09-06/U5gxfZXk8pwgOXCE.avif","alt":"","loading":"lazy"}]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["If you don't set this option in your ",["$","code",null,{"children":"nx.json"}]," file, our generators will now prompt you as to how we should set:"]}],["$","ul",null,{"children":[["$","li",null,{"children":["the name of your project (as in the project's ",["$","code",null,{"children":"project.json"}]," file)"]}],["$","li",null,{"children":"where to put the project in your filesystem"}]]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["$","img",null,{"src":"/documentation/blog/images/2023-09-06/eBwA23cwcMv8V5zF.avif","alt":"","loading":"lazy"}]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["You can avoid the extra prompt above by setting the ",["$","code",null,{"children":"projectNameAndRootFormat"}]," in the ",["$","code",null,{"children":"workspaceLayout"}]," category of your ",["$","code",null,{"children":"nx.json"}]," file, or by providing it in in your generate command:"]}],["$","$Ld",null,{"fileName":"","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"> nx g app my-app --projectNameAndRootFormat=as-provided\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["This will change some of the default behaviors of our generators and for new Nx workspaces. By default, new workspaces will be set to ",["$","code",null,{"children":"as-provided"}],", so longtime Nx users might notice that if they create a new workspace and start generating apps or libs, that they will now get generated at the root of the workspace instead of inside the ",["$","code",null,{"children":"apps/"}]," or ",["$","code",null,{"children":"libs/"}]," directory like they used to."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["To get the legacy behavior, be sure to change the ",["$","code",null,{"children":"projectNameAndRootFormat"}]," to ",["$","code",null,{"children":"derived"}]," and/or use the ",["$","code",null,{"children":"name"}]," option to set the name of the project, and the ",["$","code",null,{"children":"directory"}]," option to set the full path of the target directory for your new projects:"]}],["$","$Ld",null,{"fileName":"","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"> nx g app --name=my-app --directory=apps/my-app\n"}],["$","h2",null,{"id":"packaging-typescript-libraries-secondary-entrypoints-esm-cjs","className":"group scroll-mt-20","children":["Packaging TypeScript Libraries — Secondary Entrypoints, ESM, CJS",["$","$Lb",null,{"aria-hidden":"true","href":"#packaging-typescript-libraries-secondary-entrypoints-esm-cjs","children":["$","svg",null,{"xmlns":"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg","fill":"none","viewBox":"0 0 24 24","strokeWidth":1.5,"stroke":"currentColor","aria-hidden":"true","data-slot":"icon","aria-labelledby":"$undefined","className":"mb-1 ml-2 inline h-5 w-5 opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100","children":[null,["$","path",null,{"strokeLinecap":"round","strokeLinejoin":"round","d":"M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"}]]}]}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"text-center","children":[" ",["$","iframe",null,{"src":"https://www.youtube.com/embed/Vy4d0-SF5cY","title":"$undefined","width":"100%","allow":"accelerometer; autoplay; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; fullscreen","loading":"lazy","credentialless":"true","className":"rounded-lg shadow-lg"}],"$undefined"]}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Nx has always had first-class TypeScript support from the very beginning. You never really had to worry about Type Definition files being properly included or setting up TS support in the first place. But there are some aspects that can be tricky, like defining and properly exporting secondary entry points or packaging in multiple formats (ESM and CJS). That's why we did some research to help make it easier."}],["$","p",null,{"children":["The ",["$","code",null,{"children":"package.json"}]," format supports an ",["$","$Lb",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"https://nodejs.org/api/packages.html#package-entry-points","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"export field","target":"_blank","rel":"noreferrer","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}]," which is a modern alternative to the ",["$","code",null,{"children":"main"}]," property, allowing to have multiple such entry points."]}],["$","$Ld",null,{"language":"json","fileName":"","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"{\n  \"exports\": {\n    \"./package.json\": \"./package.json\",\n    \".\": \"./src/index.js\",\n    \"./foo\": \"./src/foo.js\",\n    \"./bar\": \"./src/bar.js\"\n  }\n}\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["To make it easier to maintain these, we've added two new options to the ",["$","code",null,{"children":"@nx/js"}]," package: ",["$","code",null,{"children":"additionalEntryPoints"}]," and ",["$","code",null,{"children":"generateExportsField"}],". Here's an example:"]}],["$","$Ld",null,{"fileName":"","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"// packages/my-awesome-lib/project.json\n{\n  \"name\": \"my-awesome-lib\",\n  \"targets\": {\n    \"build\": {\n      \"executor\": \"@nx/js:tsc\",\n      ...\n      \"options\": {\n        \"main\": \"packages/my-awesome-lib/src/index.ts\",\n        ...\n        \"additionalEntryPoints\": [\"packages/my-awesome-lib/src/foo.ts\"],\n        \"generateExportsField\": true\n      },\n    },\n    ...\n  }\n}\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["When building the library, the ",["$","code",null,{"children":"@nx/js:tsc"}]," executor automatically adds the correct ",["$","code",null,{"children":"exports"}]," definition to the resulting ",["$","code",null,{"children":"package.json"}],"."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["This makes it easy to maintain the ",["$","code",null,{"children":"exports"}]," field, something that becomes even more relevant if we ",["$","strong",null,{"children":"want to add add multiple formats"}],". By switching to the ",["$","code",null,{"children":"@nx/rollup:rollup"}]," executor, we can also add the ",["$","code",null,{"children":"format"}]," option and define ",["$","code",null,{"children":"esm"}]," and ",["$","code",null,{"children":"cjs"}]," as the target formats we want to have. Note we still keep the ",["$","code",null,{"children":"additionalEntryPoints"}]," and ",["$","code",null,{"children":"generateExportsField"}],"."]}],["$","$Ld",null,{"fileName":"","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"// packages/my-awesome-lib/project.json\n{\n  \"name\": \"my-awesome-lib\",\n  \"targets\": {\n    \"build\": {\n      \"executor\": \"@nx/rollup:rollup\",\n      ...\n      \"options\": {\n        \"main\": \"packages/my-awesome-lib/src/index.ts\",\n        ...\n        \"format\": [\"esm\", \"cjs\"],\n        \"additionalEntryPoints\": [\"packages/my-awesome-lib/src/foo.ts\"],\n        \"generateExportsField\": true\n      },\n    },\n    ...\n  }\n}\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["After compiling our package using ",["$","code",null,{"children":"nx build my-awesome-lib"}]," we'll get the following output in our ",["$","code",null,{"children":"dist"}]," folder."]}],["$","$Ld",null,{"fileName":"","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"my-awesome-lib\n└─ .\n   ├─ README.md\n   ├─ foo.cjs.d.ts\n   ├─ foo.cjs.js\n   ├─ foo.esm.js\n   ├─ index.cjs.d.ts\n   ├─ index.cjs.js\n   ├─ index.esm.js\n   ├─ package.json\n   └─ src\n      ├─ foo.d.ts\n      ├─ index.d.ts\n      └─ lib\n         └─ my-awesome-lib.d.ts\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["And our ",["$","code",null,{"children":"package.json"}]," will look as follows:"]}],["$","$Ld",null,{"language":"json","fileName":"","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"{\n  \"name\": \"my-awesome-lib\",\n  \"version\": \"0.0.1\",\n  ...\n  \"type\": \"commonjs\",\n  \"main\": \"./index.cjs.js\",\n  \"typings\": \"./src/index.d.ts\",\n  \"exports\": {\n    \"./package.json\": \"./package.json\",\n    \".\": {\n      \"import\": \"./index.esm.js\",\n      \"default\": \"./index.cjs.js\"\n    },\n    \"./foo\": {\n      \"import\": \"./foo.esm.js\",\n      \"default\": \"./foo.cjs.js\"\n    }\n  },\n  \"module\": \"./index.esm.js\"\n}\n"}],["$","h2",null,{"id":"high-performance-typescript-compilation-introducing-batch-mode-rustbased-dependency-resolution","className":"group scroll-mt-20","children":["High Performance TypeScript Compilation: Introducing Batch Mode & Rust-based Dependency Resolution",["$","$Lb",null,{"aria-hidden":"true","href":"#high-performance-typescript-compilation-introducing-batch-mode-rustbased-dependency-resolution","children":["$","svg",null,{"xmlns":"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg","fill":"none","viewBox":"0 0 24 24","strokeWidth":1.5,"stroke":"currentColor","aria-hidden":"true","data-slot":"icon","aria-labelledby":"$undefined","className":"mb-1 ml-2 inline h-5 w-5 opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100","children":[null,["$","path",null,{"strokeLinecap":"round","strokeLinejoin":"round","d":"M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"}]]}]}]]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["Speed is in our DNA! And TypeScript compilation can be slow which is mostly due to ",["$","code",null,{"children":"tsc"}]," being a costly operation. You feel this in particular in a monorepo where you have multiple projects that are being compiled independently, thus launching dozens of processes of the ",["$","code",null,{"children":"tsc"}]," compiler."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["To help with this, the TypeScript team introduced ",["$","$Lb",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"https://www.typescriptlang.org/getting-started/intro/handbook/project-references.html","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"Project References","target":"_blank","rel":"noreferrer","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}]," which performs incremental compilation by caching intermediate results. However this comes with some ",["$","$Lb",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"https://www.typescriptlang.org/getting-started/intro/handbook/project-references.html#caveats-for-project-references","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"caveats you need to be aware of","target":"_blank","rel":"noreferrer","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}]," and it can be intense to maintain by hand on a large codebase."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["We wanted to make it transparent though and not something the user needs to worry about. That's why we introduced ",["$","$Lb",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/showcase/benchmarks/tsc-batch-mode","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"\"Batch Mode\"","target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}],". Batch mode is still experimental, and you can enable it for any project using the ",["$","code",null,{"children":"@nx/js:tsc"}]," executor by adding the environment variable ",["$","code",null,{"children":"NX_BATCH_MODE=true"}],":"]}],["$","$Ld",null,{"fileName":"","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"> NX_BATCH_MODE=true nx run-many --target=build\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["When enabling batch mode, Nx leverages the underlying ",["$","$Lb",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/features/explore-graph","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"project graph","target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}]," to generate TypeScript project references behind the scenes for you, to fully leverage TS incremental building. The results are amazing. According to ",["$","$Lb",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"https://github.com/nrwl/large-ts-monorepo","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"our benchmarks","target":"_blank","rel":"noreferrer","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}],", batch mode has the potential to speed up Typescript compilation by up to 5x for large monorepos."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["$","img",null,{"src":"/documentation/blog/images/2023-09-06/givRGKA05v55ICoC.avif","alt":"","loading":"lazy"}]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["In addition to batch mode, the processing of Typescript dependencies is ",["$","strong",null,{"children":"now using a faster and more accurate Rust implementation"}],". This should be transparent to most users — aside from your monorepo becoming even faster!"]}],["$","h2",null,{"id":"new-nx-plugin-playwright","className":"group scroll-mt-20","children":["NEW Nx PLUGIN: Playwright",["$","$Lb",null,{"aria-hidden":"true","href":"#new-nx-plugin-playwright","children":["$","svg",null,{"xmlns":"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg","fill":"none","viewBox":"0 0 24 24","strokeWidth":1.5,"stroke":"currentColor","aria-hidden":"true","data-slot":"icon","aria-labelledby":"$undefined","className":"mb-1 ml-2 inline h-5 w-5 opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100","children":[null,["$","path",null,{"strokeLinecap":"round","strokeLinejoin":"round","d":"M13.19 8.688a4.5 4.5 0 0 1 1.242 7.244l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 1-6.364-6.364l1.757-1.757m13.35-.622 1.757-1.757a4.5 4.5 0 0 0-6.364-6.364l-4.5 4.5a4.5 4.5 0 0 0 1.242 7.244"}]]}]}]]}],["$","p",null,{"children":"A new Nx plugin has appeared!!"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["$","img",null,{"src":"/documentation/blog/images/2023-09-06/QBwv5NKpT9tp2-8E.avif","alt":"","loading":"lazy"}]}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Nx now supports Playwright as an e2e option alongside our long-standing support for Cypress!"}],["$","p",null,{"children":"First off, this means we've added Playwright as an option when generating new frontend applications:"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["$","img",null,{"src":"/documentation/blog/images/2023-09-06/kvqmp9n2pkivb8ba.avif","alt":"","loading":"lazy"}]}],["$","p",null,{"children":["If you want to add playwright to an existing project in your workspace you can also do that now by installing the plugin (here using ",["$","code",null,{"children":"npm"}],"):"]}],["$","$Ld",null,{"fileName":"","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"npm add -D @nx/playwright\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["Then running our new generator to add playwright to your project (here I'm adding it to my application called ",["$","code",null,{"children":"my-app"}],"):"]}],["$","$Ld",null,{"fileName":"","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"nx g @nx/playwright:configuration --project=my-app\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["This will setup playwright for you, and add an ",["$","code",null,{"children":"e2e"}]," target to your project using playwright!"]}],["$","h2",null,{"id":"new-integration-netlifys-improved-monorepo-experience","className":"group scroll-mt-20","children":["NEW INTEGRATION: Netlify's Improved Monorepo Experience",["$","$Lb",null,{"aria-hidden":"true","href":"#new-integration-netlifys-improved-monorepo-experience","children":["$","svg",null,{"xmlns":"http://www.w3.org/2000/svg","fill":"none","viewBox":"0 0 24 24","strokeWidth":1.5,"stroke":"currentColor","aria-hidden":"true","data-slot":"icon","aria-labelledby":"$undefined","className":"mb-1 ml-2 inline h-5 w-5 opacity-0 builds are monorepos, with the majority leveraging Nx and ",["$","$Lb",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"https://lerna.js.org/","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"Lerna","target":"_blank","rel":"noreferrer","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}],". Recognizing this trend, Netlify has focused on enhancing the setup and deployment experiences for monorepo projects. In particular they worked on an \"automatic monorepo detection\" feature. When you connect your project to GitHub, Netlify automatically detects if it's part of a monorepo, reads the relevant settings, and pre-configures your project. This eliminates the need for manual setup. This feature also extends to local development via the Netlify CLI."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":[["$","$Lb",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"https://twitter.com/juristr","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"Juri Strumpflohner","target":"_blank","rel":"noreferrer","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}]," from the Nx team sits down with ",["$","$Lb",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"https://twitter.com/luka5c0m","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":"Lukas Holzer","target":"_blank","rel":"noreferrer","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}]," from Netlify to discuss what monorepos are, when you might need them, their challenges and benefits, and the tooling support Nx provides. But it's not just talk; they also walk you through setting up a new Nx monorepo, adding Netlify Edge functions, and deploying it all to Netlify — both via the website and locally through the newly improved Netlify CLI."]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"text-center","children":[" Nx standalone applications. These were designed as a way of using Nx features in a way where you didn't need to opt into a monorepo setup."}],["$","p",null,{"children":"Ever since we introduced these, we've had requests from the community to add a generator to convert your standalone workspace to a monorepo setup — to support repos that grew to emcompass more than just the 1 application."}],["$","p",null,{"children":["This is where our new ",["$","$Lb",null,{"prefetch":false,"href":"/nx-api/workspace/generators/convert-to-monorepo","passHref":true,"replace":false,"scroll":true,"shallow":false,"children":[["$","code",null,{"children":"convert-to-monorepo"}]," generator"],"target":"$undefined","rel":"$undefined","className":"text-blue-600 transition-colors ease-out hover:text-blue-700 dark:text-sky-500 dark:hover:text-sky-400"}]," comes into play!"]}],["$","$Ld",null,{"language":"shell","fileName":"","highlightLines":[],"lineGroups":{},"command":"","title":"","path":"","skipRescope":false,"enableCopy":true,"lineWrap":0,"children":"nx g convert-to-monorepo\n"}],["$","p",null,{"children":["This will identify all library projects in currently in your repo and place them in the ",["$","code",null,{"children":"libs/"}]," directory that you might expect of a typical Nx monorepo. It will also hoist your application project from the root of your workspace into the ",["$","code",null,{"children":"apps/"}]," directory."]}],["$","p",null,{"children":"When all is said and done, you scroll-mt-20","children":["NEW GENERATOR: ESLint Flat
